frameworks.name: dynamic brain network interactions
frameworks.one_line: complex brain functions emerge from interactions across distributed cortical regions

frameworks.name: noninvasive brain stimulation
frameworks.one_line: TMS and tDCS used to causally probe network nodes and remote effects

frameworks.name: multimodal neuroimaging
frameworks.one_line: fMRI, PET and EEG combined with stimulation to read out network changes

frameworks.name: network dysregulation in neuropsychiatric disease
frameworks.one_line: stroke, depression and schizophrenia framed as disorders of dynamic connectivity

core_question: How can combining noninvasive brain stimulation with neuroimaging clarify dynamic brain network interactions in health and disease?
key_assumptions: complex behaviors arise from interactions among distributed cortical regions
TMS/tDCS alter activity at stimulation site and propagate to connected nodes
neuroimaging can index stimulation-induced changes in functional connectivity
neuropsychiatric disorders involve dysregulated dynamic networks
manipulations: type of noninvasive stimulation (TMS vs tDCS)
imaging modality paired with stimulation (fMRI, PET, EEG)
task state vs disease state contexts (stroke, depression, schizophrenia)
key_findings: stimulation induces focal effects plus distributed network effects beyond the site
combined stimulation-imaging enables causal assessment of inter-regional interactions
dynamic connectivity is altered in stroke, depression and schizophrenia
interpretations: concurrent stimulation-imaging reveals causal functional roles of network components
the approach offers a route to understanding and probing network dysregulation in disease
